translated: Thank you so much.
It's true that having everyone sing along to this song like that...
We haven't really had many moments like that until now.
That's true.
We all put a lot of thought into how to make this happen.
Well...
Just like Nonokura Nonoka-san mentioned,
most of our songs usually have 'Hai! Hai!' calls.
So even with 'Sing Shine Smile,' where the lyrics say 'Let's sing together,'
many people were probably wondering, 'Is it okay for me to sing out loud?'
or 'How loud should I be singing?' I think there was a lot of hesitation.
I actually saw many people who looked that way. But since the 7th Live was 'Music World,'
we really wanted to showcase Liella! as a group that cherishes singing
and a school that truly values music.
That's why we wanted everyone to sing, and why we chose 'Sing Shine Smile' for the double encore on the final day.
Even though the lyrics say 'Let's sing together,'
and some people have been singing along for a while,
there were still many who weren't sure what to do.
We're grateful for them, but we really wanted to say, 'Please, sing with us!'
We thought so much about how to lead them to feel that singing is fun.
We wanted to encourage everyone to sing along
while still maintaining the immersive world of 'Music World.'
That's what we were aiming for.
But everyone sang so much,
and it made me incredibly happy.
Plus, with every performance,
more people realized, 'Oh, it's okay to sing!'
The voices kept getting louder with each show,
and I was so moved by how the sense of unity grew stronger.
When everyone is singing, we can see your sparkling smiles
so clearly from the stage.
Those smiles are truly wonderful.
Singing together makes us smile even more too.
It was just so wonderful.
For this live, we really wanted to connect our hearts through song more than ever.
We wanted to make it a concert where everyone could feel that.
Since we all felt that way, I'm so glad we could include songs for everyone to sing.
I definitely want to have more opportunities like that in the future.
I want to keep increasing the number of songs
where we can all sing together.
Lately, Liella! has had many songs that are more about 'showing' the performance,
like 'Aspiration' or 'Jump Into the New World.'
While we're performing those, it's more like 'Whoa!'
There wasn't really that kind of back-and-forth interaction
with the audience.
So, 'Sing Shine Smile' was a big challenge for us in a good way,
and I was so happy that everyone joined in with us.
Yeah.
I feel like...
I could really feel that connection.
We felt it too.
Mm-hmm.
I know some people might still feel hesitant or wonder if it's okay to sing,
but Sayurin always says 'Please sing along with us' before we start, right?
Since Kanon-chan says it, when she does,
I hope you'll just sing your heart out without any worries. Thank you!
Yeah.
Thank you so much.
It was so much fun.
It really was.
Moving on, this next one is from Liella! Radio name, Motocross.

Thank you. 'The first performance of "Orange no Mama de" at the 7th Live was amazing. Putting this song about the sparkle and fleeting nature of youth in the encore was just unfair. Especially on the final day, I couldn't even wave my penlight; I just stood there soaking in that happy moment.'
Thank you very much.
Thank you!
Thank you.
Next is from Liella! Radio name, Amatta Neji.
Thank you. 'I attended every single performance of the 7th Live. Thank you!'
Thank you so much.
Thank you!
'"Orange no Mama de" is the perfect song for an encore.'
'I couldn't watch the part where everyone gathered to strike the same pose as the jacket art without crying.'
'Thank you for the best youth ever.'
That's what they wrote.
Thank you so much.
Thank you!
I love it.
"Orange no Mama de."
I love it so much.
We all love it.
It really is a great song.
